name: Reserve Bank of India
summary: ALL THREE UNKNOWNS RESOLVED at Malhotra's 12 noon presser. Repo HELD 5.25%, neutral stance, 6-0 unanimous (Ram Singh dissented on stance — argued for accommodative). FY27 CPI 4.6% (lower than consensus 4.7-5.1%, ceasefire partially walked back revision). FY27 GDP 6.9% (down from 7.6% but ABOVE consensus 6.5-6.8%). NOP cap April 10 deadline STANDS, no extension — but Malhotra called curbs 'temporary' and 'reactive,' banks already unwound ~75% ($30B of ~$40B). Most dovish forward guidance since Malhotra became Governor: 'Interest rates are likely to remain low in the medium to long term.' June 25bps cut pathway OPEN if Brent stays below $100.

signals.content: RBI created intervention space by changing maturity profile of short dollar forwards, reducing near-term delivery obligations to take on fresh short positions.
RBI sold $18-20B in single week to cushion rupee. Revived heavy pre-market intervention through state-run banks.
RBI announced ₹1 trillion ($10.9B) bond purchases from open market, adding to screen-based purchases. Supporting both currency and bond markets simultaneously.

RBI net short dollar position approaching $100B — effectively betting crisis resolves before reserves run out. Intervening mainly in forward segment rather than spot. Forex reserves dropped $7B to $709.75B. Banking liquidity turned deficit. Repo rate held at 5.25% despite inflation pressure.

RBI imposed emergency NOP-INR hard cap of $100 million on ALL authorized dealer banks regardless of balance sheet size (March 28), compliance deadline April 10. Previously banks could maintain NOP limits up to 25% of capital. This is explicitly anti-speculation and constitutes a form of capital flow management — not full capital controls but significant tightening of forex market architecture. Escalation ladder: conventional intervention (selling dollars) → regulatory caps (NOP limit, this step) → full capital controls (next if NOP cap doesn't hold). Forex reserves dropped to $698.35 billion (below $700B, down $30.14B in 3 weeks of March from ATH $728.49B pre-war). Gold component dropped $13.5B in one week. Net short dollar forward position approaching $100 billion. (Sources: Business Standard, RBI circular, Bloomberg)

NOP position estimates revised dramatically upward: $25-50B (Bloomberg: '$30B unwinding looms'), not $10-18B previously estimated. Banks met RBI Saturday, asking for 3 months to comply (not April 10). Citing MTM losses and 'one-sided panic' risk. Rupee rallied in NDF on Monday as banks began selling dollars. Most positions in 1-3 month bucket. (Sources: Bloomberg, Business Standard, MarketScreener/Reuters, TradingView/Reuters)

EVENING UPDATE — NOP CAP FAILED WITHIN 4 HOURS: The NOP cap's mechanical effect (forcing banks to sell dollars) drove the rupee to 93.56 at open — a 125 paise rally. But gains evaporated by afternoon as oil surged to $115+ on Trump's FT interview. Rupee hit record intraday low 95.22 before closing ~95.07. MUFG Research: 'India — RBI limits on INR onshore positions: Can't stop this train.' Business Standard: 'Temporary relief for Rupee, not a trend reversal.' The RBI's escalation ladder is now effectively exhausted: conventional intervention ($30B+ burned) → NOP regulatory cap (failed in 4 hours). Next steps would be: emergency rate hike (politically radioactive, would crash equities further), outright capital controls (last resort), or IMF-style measures. The speed of reversal (4 hours) suggests the structural depreciation pressure from $115 oil + $14.8B FPI outflows overwhelms any regulatory intervention. (Sources: MUFG Research, Business Standard, FXStreet, Bloomberg)

NOP cap FAILED within 4 hours on March 30 — rupee opened at 93.56 on forced dollar selling, then collapsed to record 95.22 as oil surged above $115 on Trump FT interview. MUFG Research: 'Can't stop this train.' RBI escalation ladder now exhausted: spot intervention → forward intervention → NOP cap (all tried, all failed). Next options: emergency rate hike or capital controls — both politically radioactive. Banks pushing back on NOP cap, requesting 3-month extension vs April 10 deadline. No RBI response. OECD flagged possible Q2 rate hike. Bloomberg: $30-40B positions need squaring (higher than $10-18B estimate). The NOP cap is a REGULATORY STRUCTURAL CHANGE — doesn't reverse even if oil drops. CONFIRMED.

NOP cap deadline April 10 — NO EXTENSION GRANTED despite bank pushback. Banks warning of $30-40B in forced unwinding (Bloomberg). RBI DID grant separate 3-month extension on capital market exposure norms (deferred from April 1 to July 1) — showing flexibility exists, but NOP cap serves different, more urgent purpose (rupee defense). India 10Y GOI breached 7.00% for first time since July 2024, driven by: record FY27 borrowing (₹17.2 trillion gross), oil-driven inflation expectations, RBI on hold. Bank Nifty -3.8% — PSU banks hammered (Union -6.55%, Canara -5.21%, BoB -5.07%). New FY27 STT rules effective: futures 0.02%→0.05%, options 0.10%→0.15%. (Sources: Business Standard, Bloomberg, RBI, TradingEconomics)

RBI MPC meeting scheduled April 6-8. Repo rate at 5.25% (after 125bps cuts in 2025). CPI inflation at 3.21% (well below 4% target). Expected: HOLD — assess whether oil shock transitory or structural. Dilemma: imported inflation from oil above $100 vs growth slowdown. If oil stays at $100+, imported inflation pressures rise despite low domestic CPI. (Sources: RBI, Business Standard)

NOP cap deadline April 10 — no extension announced. Banks unwinding $30-40B in net open positions. Bank Nifty -3.8% on March 30, +2.3% recovery April 1. Estimated MTM losses Rs 3,000-4,000 crore across banking sector from forced position unwinding. (Sources: RBI, Business Standard)

MPC April 6-8 — 3 DAYS AWAY. Market expects hold at 5.25%. But stagflation trap deepening: oil at $109 (import bill), food inflation building from fertilizer disruption (6-9 month lag), FPI exodus accelerating (₹9,931 cr April 2), rupee under NOP cap pressure. RBI trapped: can't cut (inflation), can't hike (growth), can't stand still (credibility). NOP cap April 10 deadline unchanged. India 10Y at 7.03% (+32bps in March). ECB at 84% hike probability — global central bank hawkishness reinforcing. (Sources: RBI, Business Standard, CNBC)

RBI MPC meeting starts April 6-8. Expected hold at 5.25%. Facing 'trade-off central banks dislike most' — imported oil inflation vs growth slowdown. NOP cap deadline April 10 firm, forcing $30-40B in forex position unwinding. Rupee +1.8% to 93.10 on April 4 (largest gain since 2013) from NOP force — regulatory intervention, not organic. India 10Y at 7.11-7.13%. (Sources: RBI, Business Standard, PingTV India)

MPC DAY 1: Six-member committee headed by Governor Sanjay Malhotra convened April 6. Decision announcement April 8. Consensus: HOLD at 5.25% repo rate. Key projections: CareEdge GDP 6.7%, CPI 4.5-4.7% (if crude $90 — it's now $121). Bank of Baroda GDP 7-7.2% (20-25bp Iran war impact), CPI +50bp upside. Indian crude basket $120.84/bbl in April — well above all forecast scenarios. BusinessToday: 'West Asia war expected to weigh heavily due to surging inflation.' Policy Circle: 'growth-inflation trade-off.' El Nino conditions may weaken monsoons, adding food price risk atop oil inflation. NOP cap April 10 deadline unchanged. India 10Y at 7.11-7.13%. THREE sequential binary events this week for Indian markets.

MPC Day 2 underway, decision April 8 — consensus HOLD at 5.25% (69/71 Reuters poll economists). India 10Y GOI at 7.05% (-5bps) suggests market expects dovish hold with growth emphasis. RBI trapped: oil at $111 = inflation constraint, rupee at ₹93.04 = currency constraint, growth slowing. FPI outflows ₹1.31 lakh crore in 2026. NOP cap deadline April 10 — banks completing $30-40B dollar unwinding, urging RBI to revisit cap and flagging mark-to-market losses. Without the war, RBI would likely be cutting rates. Special Saturday trading session April 11 confirmed by NSE. Forex reserves depleted from $728.49B to ~$698B. (Sources: Business Standard, BusinessToday, Trading Economics, Reuters)

MPC DAY 2 DELIBERATIONS APRIL 7 — Decision Wednesday April 8, 10 AM IST. Six-member committee continues. Reuters poll: 69 of 71 economists see hold at 5.25%. Day 1 (April 6) speculation suggests Governor Malhotra emphasized 'growth concerns paramount' — early dovish read. India 10Y GOI eased -5 bps to 7.05% on this signal. Indian crude basket at $124/bbl is 46% above RBI's $85 February assumption. February CPI already at 3.21% (10-month high) BEFORE oil pass-through. FY27 CPI projection 4.5-5.1% — approaches 6% ceiling. Multibagg/Policy Circle consensus: hold at 5.25% with hawkish tone, June pivot if oil >$100. Asymmetric risk: dovish surprise = Bank Nifty +3-5%; hawkish surprise = -3-5%. The RBI MPC + Iran deadline collision on April 7-8 is being framed as a once-in-a-decade policy stress test.

EVENING UPDATE: MPC Day 2 deliberations underway — decision April 8 at 10 AM IST. Hold consensus overwhelming: BS 10/10, Goodreturns 23/25 (92%), Reuters 69/71. Indian crude basket $120.84/bbl (46% above RBI $85 assumption). BusinessToday raised rate HIKE question for FY27 — framing shift. ICRA's Aditi Nayar: 'remain on pause.' SBI's Ghosh flags 'super El Nino' risk. Governor Malhotra's TONE on Wednesday is the signal — Day 1 read was dovish, easing India 10Y to 7.05%. NOP cap deadline April 10 firm — $11-15B still unwinding, banks requesting 3-month extension with no RBI response. Saturday trading April 11 confirmed. Asymmetric risk: dovish surprise = Bank Nifty +3-5%; hawkish surprise = -3-5%. (Sources: Business Standard, Reuters, BusinessToday, ICRA)

RBI MPC DECISION PENDING 10 AM IST — MACRO BACKDROP DRAMATICALLY TRANSFORMED BY CEASEFIRE [REPORTED]. The RBI's first MPC meeting of FY27 concludes at 10 AM IST today. Pre-ceasefire forecasts: HOLD at 5.25% (Reuters 69/71), neutral stance, FY27 inflation revised UP to 4.5-4.7%, GDP revised DOWN to 6.5-6.8% (CareEdge 6.7%, ICRA 6.5% vs prior 7.0-7.4%). The ceasefire transforms this: Brent now $94 (vs RBI's $85 baseline, within ~$9 not the prior $25 gap), rupee strengthening, oil-driven inflation pressure dramatically reduced. BusinessToday's 'rate hike question' framing of yesterday is now dead. The case for a hawkish hold is dramatically weakened. Governor Malhotra's TONE will be the main signal. Press conference at 12 noon IST. Watch for: (a) inflation projection revisions (whether the upward revision is dampened), (b) GDP forecast cuts (whether the downward revision is partially walked back), (c) any direct mention of the Hormuz situation, (d) NOP cap extension reference (banks have requested 3-month extension).

RBI MPC DELIVERED — REPO HELD AT 5.25%, NEUTRAL STANCE RETAINED [CONFIRMED]. The first bi-monthly policy of FY27 came in line with consensus (Reuters poll 69/71 economists). The policy statement reaffirmed continued use of OMO purchases and FX swaps to maintain comfortable liquidity conditions and continued focus on transmission of prior cuts. Vote split REPORTED as unanimous 6-0 to hold with external member Ram Singh again arguing for shift to 'accommodative' stance — though this exact dissent phrasing matches the February 2026 meeting and may be a Feb carry-over in secondary sources; not yet confirmed from the RBI resolution text. The market treated the rate decision as a non-event — the morning rally was ~80% crisis-related (overnight ceasefire) and ~20% policy-related. THREE HIGH-PRIORITY UNKNOWNS reserved for the 12 noon IST Malhotra press conference: (a) exact FY27 CPI projection (pre-meeting consensus had it revised UP to 4.5-4.7%, but Brent at $94 may walk that back), (b) FY27 GDP projection (consensus 6.5-6.8% — CareEdge 6.7%, ICRA 6.5%), (c) whether the NOP cap was extended or grandfathered ahead of the April 10 deadline (single most important banking-sector unknown). Sources: Multibagg post-decision summary, GoodReturns live, BusinessToday.

Governor Malhotra's April 8 12 noon IST press conference resolved three high-priority unknowns: (1) FY27 CPI projected at 4.6% (Q1 4.0%, Q2 4.4%, Q3 5.2% peak, Q4 4.7%) — lower than pre-meeting consensus of 4.7-5.1%, ceasefire (Brent at $94 vs $85 baseline) partially walked back larger revision. (2) FY27 GDP projected at 6.9% (Q1 6.8%, Q2 6.7%, Q3 7.0%, Q4 7.2%) — down from 7.6% but higher than consensus 6.5-6.8%. (3) NOP cap April 10 deadline STANDS, no extension — but Malhotra called curbs 'temporary' and 'reactive,' 'should not be considered a structural change.' Banks already unwound ~75% ($30B of ~$40B), remaining ~$10B manageable. (Source: Upstox, BusinessToday, Bloomberg, The Print, MarketScreener, FreePressJournal)

Most dovish forward guidance since Malhotra became Governor: 'Interest rates are likely to remain low in the medium to long term.' Vote split: 6-0 unanimous on hold at 5.25%. Ram Singh dissented on STANCE — argued for shift from 'neutral' to 'accommodative.' If oil stays below $100, RBI has room for 25bps cut at June MPC. (Source: The Print, Upstox, BusinessToday)

edges.context: RBI held 5.25%, dovish forward guidance. FY27 CPI 4.6%, GDP 6.9%. NOP cap April 10 STANDS but 'temporary.' India 10Y collapsed to 6.91%. June 25bps cut pathway open if oil stays below $100.
NOP cap April 10 deadline STANDS — Malhotra called curbs 'temporary' and 'reactive.' Banks unwound ~75% ($30B of ~$40B), remaining ~$10B manageable. INR at ₹92.55 strengthening.
Dovish hold + dovish forward guidance. India 10Y collapsed to 6.91%. Bank Nifty +5.0% on rate cut hopes. June 25bps cut pathway open — NIM expansion narrative strengthening.

Brent at $96.75 — below $100 for first time. Oil constraint on RBI easing significantly. FY27 CPI projection 4.6% (lower than consensus) reflects ceasefire oil relief. If oil stays below $100, June 25bps cut becomes base case.

trigger_points.mechanism: FURTHER AWAY after dovish forward guidance ('interest rates likely to remain low in medium to long term'). June 25bps CUT is now more likely than any hike if oil stays below $100. Would signal panic mode. Would crash equities but might stabilize rupee temporarily. Last resort.

trigger_points.condition: RBI MPC surprises with rate hike (April 6-8)
trigger_points.mechanism: FURTHER AWAY — MPC held 5.25% with most dovish forward guidance since Malhotra became Governor. June 25bps CUT is now base case if oil stays below $100. Ram Singh dissented toward accommodative, not hawkish. Hike scenario effectively off the table unless oil spikes back above $110+ AND ceasefire collapses.
